page: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the system allocates 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 960 GB NVMe SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU is present.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core and memory capabilities alongside some multi-threaded limitations. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks and the PassMark memory score both rank in the top 25%. However, multi-threaded performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores falling in the middle 50%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput are weaker, landing in the bottom 25%.

The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance class suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU pinning, given its shared CPU allocation. While single-threaded execution is highly efficient, the shared CPU model and lower multi-threaded decompression metrics represent a tradeoff for highly parallelized compute tasks. Database operations also show lower relative performance. This server is best suited for memory-heavy applications, caching layers, and moderate-scale web applications that benefit from a large memory footprint and fast local NVMe storage.
description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB of memory per core, and includes a 960 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory write bandwidth, though its multi-core scaling and database operations show relative weakness. The shared CPU allocation and high memory-to-vCPU ratio make this instance suitable for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, and general-purpose workloads that require a substantial memory footprint without demanding dedicated, continuous multi-core compute resources.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd is an AMD EPYC Genoa server with 4 shared vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance and high memory density.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd features 4 shared AMD Genoa vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD Genoa processors, high memory density, and fast local NVMe SSD storage.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
